frameworks.name: neural toggle switch
frameworks.one_line: circuit switch gating fear-to-anxiety and approach-vs-avoid decisions

frameworks.name: conditioned threat / Pavlovian fear
frameworks.one_line: classical threat-learning framework underlying defensive responses

frameworks.name: cost-benefit decision
frameworks.one_line: active avoidance weighed against reward magnitude and effort cost

core_question: How do brain circuits for conditioned threat, active avoidance, and reward intersect to produce approach-versus-avoid decisions?
key_assumptions: active avoidance decisions integrate threat, cost, and reward signals
fear and anxiety are dissociable states linked by a circuit transition
shared neural substrates can be probed across these domains
key_findings: review consolidates behavioral and circuit evidence on active avoidance and its threat interactions
active avoidance excess co-occurs across multiple psychiatric conditions
interpretations: a neural toggle switch model can explain fear-to-anxiety transitions and approach-vs-avoid arbitration
a unified experimental approach across threat, avoidance, and reward is needed to understand pathology
limitations_noted: threat, avoidance, and reward have largely been studied in silos
